title: malus apiola
intro: Malus apiola, also known as the Apple of the Alps, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family. The medicinal properties of Malus apiola include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In terms of herbal preparations, Malus apiola is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
